Perimenopause support

Balance. Thrive. Repeat.

Balanced Feminine Path helps you understand the changes happening in midlife and gives you practical support to feel clear, steady, and more like yourself again.

Shop Digital Tools Explore Free Resources
  • Perimenopause symptom support
  • Brain fog and focus struggles
  • Mental and physical fatigue support
A strong place to start

Track your symptoms.

Use the tracker, reset plans, and guided tools to notice patterns, prepare questions, and stop guessing your way through symptoms.

Start with the Symptom Tracker
If this sounds familiar

It may be perimenopause.

Many women reach midlife feeling scattered, flat, overstimulated, mentally drained, physically tired, or unlike themselves. Hormone changes can affect focus, energy, sleep, mood, and emotional steadiness in ways that feel confusing when no one has explained the full picture.

◌

Brain fog and forgetfulness

You lose words, forget simple things, or feel slower than you used to.

◌

Mental and physical fatigue

You feel worn down, heavy, or drained even when you are trying to rest and keep going.

◌

Mood swings, overwhelm, and stress load

You feel irritable, tearful, anxious, or stretched thin faster than before.

◌

Focus struggles that feel unfamiliar

You start wondering if it is perimenopause, burnout, ADHD, or some mix of all three.

How Balanced Feminine Path helps

Simple symptom support.

Understand what is changing

Track symptoms, spot patterns, and put words to what your body is doing.

Steady your day

Use simple tools for sleep, food, energy, morning rhythm, and nervous system support.

Walk into care prepared

Bring better notes, sharper questions, and more confidence into doctor conversations.

Start with the support that fits

Choose your support.

These products give you structure without making your life harder.

Best first step

Perimenopause Symptom Tracker and Doctor Appointment Checklist

Track symptoms, spot patterns, and walk into doctor visits with cleaner notes and better questions.

View product
Daily rhythm

Perimenopause Morning Routine for Fatigue and Focus

Build a steadier start to the day when brain fog, fatigue, and stressful mornings keep knocking you off course.

View product
Sleep and cognition

Perimenopause Brain Fog and Sleep Guide

Track sleep disruption, mental fog, and hot flash patterns with a practical plan you can use right away.

View product
Food and energy

Perimenopause Meal Plan for Energy and Mood

Use a simpler food plan for energy crashes, cravings, mood dips, and steadier meals through the week.

View product
Full reset

4-Week Perimenopause Reset

Bring symptoms, food, sleep, daily habits, and reflection into one guided four-week reset.

View product
What you will learn here

Name what you feel.

Balanced Feminine Path centers perimenopause with extra attention on the symptoms that can make women feel confused, dismissed, or unlike themselves.

Brain fog and focus

Clarity around forgetfulness, scattered thinking, and ADHD-like symptoms in midlife.

Mood and emotional regulation

Support for irritability, anxiety, tears, and feeling stretched too thin.

Sleep and nervous system load

Tools for night waking, restless sleep, and the stress loop that keeps symptoms going.

Body literacy

Help noticing patterns, tracking symptoms, and preparing for better care conversations.

Midlife identity and self-trust

Support for women who want to feel steady, informed, and more at home in their bodies again.

About Dr Oluwaseun Ade-Alade

Meet Dr Oluwaseun.

Dr Oluwaseun Ade-Alade created Balanced Feminine Path to help women make sense of midlife changes with calm guidance, useful tools, and support they can apply in real life.

This work gives women a better way to track symptoms, rebuild daily rhythm, and prepare for stronger conversations with their healthcare providers.

Learn More
A clear next step

Start here.

The content on Balanced Feminine Path supports education and personal reflection. It does not replace medical care. Always consult your doctor or another qualified healthcare professional about symptoms, treatment, supplements, or medication.
